title: dysoxylum pilosum
intro: Dysoxylum pilosum, also known as the Philippine rosewood or Philippine padauk, is a species of plant in the family Meliaceae. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties, which are derived from its bark, leaves, and roots. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its ability to reduce inflammation and promote healing. Dysoxylum pilosum can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used in traditional medicine to treat a range of ailments, with the bark being used to make a medicinal oil.
